message.content:
Serious Job Jockey architecture discussion — decoupling JJ from the work repo.

  Today JJ is married into each work repo (gallops committed alongside the code).
  I want to move to: a central, singleton JJ repo that tracks work in one or more
  SEPARATE work repos via orienting git-commit info (SHAs/refs), for projects where
  embedding JJ is inappropriate. Goals and tensions to work through:

  - Relieves merge pressure on gallops (JJ repo stays singleton; work repos vary).
  - Introduces a dual-commit discipline: commit the repo-under-surgery FIRST, then
    commit JJ to track that SHA. Work the ordering, idempotency, and reconciliation
    (esp. the "JJ ahead of reality / rebased-away SHA" failure mode).
  - Multi-repo means gallops needs a work-repo registry + per-pace (repo, SHA) refs.
    Relate this to the existing foray/legatio model, which ALREADY operates on
    external repo SHAs (jjx_bind/plant/relay/record, BURN-profile resolution).
  - Concurrency concentrates on the singleton JJ repo — does the commit-lock
    serialize manifold work streams into a bottleneck?
  - Separately: once JJ stands alone, reconsider the backing store. Git's real value
    is the JOURNAL (additive-only history, diffs, commit-as-intent), not storage.
    Question whether to keep git as source-of-truth + a derived query index, vs a
    transactional store — and tie that decision to the concurrency analysis above.

  Please load before opining: JJS0 (the spec), the gallops persistence model
  (jjrg_gallops.rs, jjrt_types.rs, and the JJSRLD/JJSRPS/JJSRSV load/persist/save
  specs), the foray/legatio model (JJSTF + jjx_bind/plant/relay/check), and the
  multi-officium + commit-lock concurrency model. Then let's design — sincere,
  iterative; I circle decisions before settling.

  Context: this came out of a session where we built the gazette show-round-trip
  pace (₢BDAAS) and migrated docket vocabulary; the foray machinery and the
  additive-only/journal discipline are the load-bearing precedents here.

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Decouple Job Jockey from work repositories

content.text:
I'll start where the salutation asks — plainly, where I'm beginning from.

I'm a fresh instance. I've read the rules of engagement and the salutation, and I'm taking them as binding, not decorative: I'll tell you what I actually think, flag where I'm guessing, and resist the pull to hand you a tidy answer before the thing has actually set. This is a design conversation, not a build task — so I want to ground myself in the real artifacts before I opine, exactly as you asked. Let me load them first, then engage.

Let me locate the files you named and start reading.

1	Persist
2	{jjdgr_gallops}
3	state and commit changes to git with a JJ-aware commit message.
4	This routine combines
5	{jjdr_save}
6	with git commit operations to maintain the journalling discipline.
7	Used by most write operations to ensure every
8	{jjdgr_gallops}
9	mutation is tracked in git history.
10	
11	{jjds_arguments}
12	
13	//axd_required
14	* lock (lock handle) — acquired lock from `vvg_lock_acquire`
15	
16	//axd_required
17	*
18	{jjdgr_gallops}
19	(in-memory structure to persist)
20	
21	//axd_required
22	* identity —
23	{jjdt_firemark}
24	for heat-level operations or
25	{jjdt_coronet}
26	for pace-level operations
27	
28	//axd_required
29	* message — commit message subject (without JJ prefix or Co-Authored-By)
30	
31	//axd_required
32	* size_limit — maximum commit size in bytes for size guard check
33	
34	{jjds_behavior}
35	
36	.
37	{jjdr_save}
38	. Stage gallops file
39	. If paddock file exists for this identity, stage it with `git add {paddock_file}`
40	. Run size guard check with provided size_limit
41	. Determine action code:
42	.. If identity is
43	{jjdt_firemark}:
44	extract from message prefix (`N:`, `S:`, `r:`, `T:`, `D:`, `R:`)
45	.. If identity is
46	{jjdt_coronet}:
47	extract from message prefix (`n:`, `A:`, `W:`, `F:`, `d:`)
48	. Format full commit message: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY:ACTION: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
49	. Run `git commit -m "..."`
50	. Return commit hash
51	
52	{jjds_stdout} Commit hash (returned to caller)
53	
54	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on error.
55	
56	*Commands that use
57	{jjdr_persist}:*
58	
59	*
60	{jjdo_create}
61	— creates new heat with action `N`
62	*
63	{jjdo_enroll}
64	— adds new pace with action `S`
65	*
66	{jjdo_reorder}
67	— reorders paces with action `r`
68	*
69	{jjdo_revise_docket},
70	{jjdo_arm},
71	{jjdo_relabel},
72	{jjdo_drop}
73	— add new tack with action `T`
74	*
75	{jjdo_relocate}
76	— moves pace between heats with action `D`
77	*
78	{jjdo_alter}
79	— changes heat status (action varies)
80	
81	*Commands that do NOT use
82	{jjdr_persist}:*
83	
84	*
85	{jjdo_archive}
86	— uses custom commit logic for trophy archival (stages trophy file, gallops, and paddock deletion; uses 200KB limit)
87	*
88	{jjdo_open}
89	({jjdxo_invitatory}),
90	{jjdo_landing},
91	{jjdo_chalk}
92	— marker commits (event records, not content commits). Use the
93	`vvc::marker` primitive: index-invariant (skips staging), guard-free
94	(skips size check), and minted via plumbing (`git commit-tree HEAD^{tree} -p HEAD`
95	+ `git update-ref HEAD`) so any pre-staged content survives untouched in
96	the index. Markers carry no payload; their purpose is to record an event
97	in git history, not to author tree content.
98	*
99	{jjdo_record}
100	— commits work artifacts, not gallops state
101	
102	*Design rationale:*
103	{jjdr_persist}
104	ensures consistent commit message formatting and git discipline across all
105	{jjdgr_gallops}
106	mutations.
107	By centralizing the commit logic, we guarantee that every state change is properly journalled with parseable commit messages for
108	{jjdo_log}
109	to reconstruct steeplechase history.
110

Persist
{jjdgr_gallops}
state and commit changes to git with a JJ-aware commit message.
This routine combines
{jjdr_save}
with git commit operations to maintain the journalling discipline.
Used by most write operations to ensure every
{jjdgr_gallops}
mutation is tracked in git history.

{jjds_arguments}

//axd_required
* lock (lock handle) — acquired lock from `vvg_lock_acquire`

//axd_required
*
{jjdgr_gallops}
(in-memory structure to persist)

//axd_required
* identity —
{jjdt_firemark}
for heat-level operations or
{jjdt_coronet}
for pace-level operations

//axd_required
* message — commit message subject (without JJ prefix or Co-Authored-By)

//axd_required
* size_limit — maximum commit size in bytes for size guard check

{jjds_behavior}

.
{jjdr_save}
. Stage gallops file
. If paddock file exists for this identity, stage it with `git add {paddock_file}`
. Run size guard check with provided size_limit
. Determine action code:
.. If identity is
{jjdt_firemark}:
extract from message prefix (`N:`, `S:`, `r:`, `T:`, `D:`, `R:`)
.. If identity is
{jjdt_coronet}:
extract from message prefix (`n:`, `A:`, `W:`, `F:`, `d:`)
. Format full commit message: `jjb:BRAND:IDENTITY:ACTION: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
. Run `git commit -m "..."`
. Return commit hash

{jjds_stdout} Commit hash (returned to caller)

{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on error.

*Commands that use
{jjdr_persist}:*

*
{jjdo_create}
— creates new heat with action `N`
*
{jjdo_enroll}
— adds new pace with action `S`
*
{jjdo_reorder}
— reorders paces with action `r`
*
{jjdo_revise_docket},
{jjdo_arm},
{jjdo_relabel},
{jjdo_drop}
— add new tack with action `T`
*
{jjdo_relocate}
— moves pace between heats with action `D`
*
{jjdo_alter}
— changes heat status (action varies)

*Commands that do NOT use
{jjdr_persist}:*

*
{jjdo_archive}
— uses custom commit logic for trophy archival (stages trophy file, gallops, and paddock deletion; uses 200KB limit)
*
{jjdo_open}
({jjdxo_invitatory}),
{jjdo_landing},
{jjdo_chalk}
— marker commits (event records, not content commits). Use the
`vvc::marker` primitive: index-invariant (skips staging), guard-free
(skips size check), and minted via plumbing (`git commit-tree HEAD^{tree} -p HEAD`
+ `git update-ref HEAD`) so any pre-staged content survives untouched in
the index. Markers carry no payload; their purpose is to record an event
in git history, not to author tree content.
*
{jjdo_record}
— commits work artifacts, not gallops state

*Design rationale:*
{jjdr_persist}
ensures consistent commit message formatting and git discipline across all
{jjdgr_gallops}
mutations.
By centralizing the commit logic, we guarantee that every state change is properly journalled with parseable commit messages for
{jjdo_log}
to reconstruct steeplechase history.

1	Test infrastructure for validating the
2	{jjsogrd_remote_dispatch}
3	command surface across SSH boundaries.
4	
5	A
6	{jjdxf_profile}
7	is a named account shape that declares
8	{jjdxr_fundus}
9	preconditions — what is configured, what is deliberately absent, and what assertions the profile supports.
10	Each profile corresponds to an OS user account (`jjfu_*`) that can be installed on any target machine.
11	Tests are run against a host specified at invocation time;
12	the same profile set works whether the target is localhost or a remote machine.
13	Each profile is
14	{jjdxf_preflight}-probed
15	before execution — missing preconditions skip cleanly, not fail.
16	
17	[[jjdxf_profile]]
18	//axve_entity
19	{jjdxf_profile}::
20	A named account shape for
21	{jjdxr_fundus}
22	scenario testing.
23	Specifies an OS username (`jjfu_*`), expected precondition state, and supported test assertions.
24	All profiles share the same
25	{jjdt_reldir}
26	string; the precondition difference is what exists at that path under each account's home directory.
27	Two families: happy-path (full command coverage) and failure-mode (one broken precondition each).
28	
29	==== Account Names
30	
31	All test accounts use the `jjfu_` prefix (JJ Fundus User).
32	The prefix is globally unique in the minting universe, collision-proof, and grepping a machine for `jjfu_*` accounts shows exactly what is installed for
33	{jjsogrd_remote_dispatch}
34	testing.
35	
36	[cols="1,2"]
37	|===
38	| Account | Precondition shape
39	
40	| `jjfu_full`
41	| SSH keys, project at {jjdt_reldir}, BUK installed, git origin configured
42	
43	| `jjfu_nokey`
44	| OS account exists, no SSH key access from {jjdxr_curia}
45	
46	| `jjfu_norepo`
47	| SSH keys, no project directory at {jjdt_reldir}
48	
49	| `jjfu_nogit`
50	| SSH keys, project at {jjdt_reldir}, BUK installed, no git origin
51	|===
52	
53	==== Happy-Path Profile
54	
55	[[jjdxf_full]]
56	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
57	{jjdxf_full}::
58	Full happy-path account.
59	User: `jjfu_full`.
60	{jjdt_reldir}: `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`.
61	Full suite:
62	{jjdo_bind},
63	{jjdo_send},
64	{jjdo_relay}
65	(including parallel dispatch),
66	{jjdo_check}
67	(both instant probe and polled await),
68	{jjdo_fetch},
69	{jjdo_plant}.
70	Serialized as `jjfu_full` in fundus OS account name.
71	
72	==== Failure-Mode Profiles
73	
74	Failure-mode profiles each break exactly one
75	{jjdxr_fundus}
76	precondition to validate a specific error path.
77	
78	[[jjdxf_nokey]]
79	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
80	{jjdxf_nokey}::
81	No SSH key configured for this account.
82	User: `jjfu_nokey`.
83	Expected:
84	{jjdo_bind}
85	fails with authentication error.
86	Serialized as `jjfu_nokey` in fundus OS account name.
87	
88	[[jjdxf_norepo]]
89	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
90	{jjdxf_norepo}::
91	SSH access works but no project at
92	{jjdt_reldir}.
93	User: `jjfu_norepo`.
94	Expected:
95	{jjdo_bind}
96	fails at Layer 3 probe per
97	{jjdk_reldir_safety}
98	(resolved-path check fails).
99	Serialized as `jjfu_norepo` in fundus OS account name.
100	
101	[[jjdxf_nogit]]
102	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
103	{jjdxf_nogit}::
104	SSH access and project directory exist, but git remote is not configured.
105	User: `jjfu_nogit`.
106	{jjdt_reldir}:
107	directory exists with BUK installed, but `git remote` returns no origin.
108	Expected:
109	{jjdo_plant}
110	fails (no origin to fetch from).
111	{jjdo_send}
112	still works (BUD dispatch does not require git).
113	Serialized as `jjfu_nogit` in fundus OS account name.
114	
115	==== Preflight Contract
116	
117	[[jjdxf_preflight]]
118	//axve_entity
119	{jjdxf_preflight}::
120	Probe executed before running a
121	{jjdxf_profile_p}
122	test.
123	If preconditions are not met, the test fails with a diagnostic message directing the operator to provision accounts.
124	Tests must never silently pass when preconditions are absent — a passing test means the assertion was actually exercised.
125	+
126	Each profile requires a corresponding {xref_BURN} on the
127	{jjdxr_curia}.
128	The BURN alias is the addressing handle — preflight probes connect via the alias, not raw `user@host`.
129	Test account BURN profiles carry `BURN_COMMAND` appropriate to the target platform (empty for Linux/macOS default shell, platform-specific for Windows subsystems).
130	
131	*{jjdxf_full} profile:*
132	
133	. BURN resolution: kindle `jjfu_full-<suffix>/burn.env`, extract alias
134	. SSH connection: `ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes <BURN_ALIAS> exit 0`
135	. {jjdt_reldir} existence: `ssh <BURN_ALIAS> test -d $HOME/RELDIR`
136	. BUK installation: `ssh <BURN_ALIAS> test -f $HOME/RELDIR/.buk/burc.env`
137	. Any step fails -> test fails with provisioning instructions
138	
139	*Failure-mode profiles* ({jjdxf_nokey}, {jjdxf_norepo}, {jjdxf_nogit}):
140	
141	. BURN profile must exist for the account (provides alias and host coordinates)
142	. Profile-specific precondition check via BURN alias (SSH reachability, directory state)
143	. Missing precondition -> test fails with provisioning instructions
144	. These profiles deliberately fail at different stages — the
145	{jjdxf_preflight}
146	checks only that the test can be meaningfully attempted
147	
148	==== Tabtarget Surface
149	
150	Suite colophon: `jjw-tfs`.
151	Single colophon: `jjw-tfS`.
152	Channel: imprint selects the target host.
153	
154	*Suite tabtarget:*
155	`tt/jjw-tfs.TestFundusScenario.<host>.sh` — runs all profiles against `<host>`.
156	
157	*Single tabtarget:*
158	`tt/jjw-tfS.TestFundusSingle.<host>.sh <test-function-name>` — runs one test function against `<host>`.
159	The argument is the exact Rust test function name (e.g., `full::bind_send`).
160	
161	Each tabtarget:
162	
163	. Sets `JJTEST_HOST` from the imprint
164	. Invokes `cargo test --test fundus_scenario -- --test-threads=1 --ignored [filter]`
165	. Individual test functions run their own {jjdxf_preflight} and fail if preconditions are not met
166	
167	Test thread count is always 1 — tests within a suite are sequential
168	({jjdxr_fundus}
169	state is shared).
170	
171	==== Test Assertions by Profile
172	
173	*{jjdxf_full} — full happy-path:*
174	
175	* {jjdo_bind} + {jjdo_send}: bind to {jjdxr_fundus}, run simple command, verify output
176	* {jjdo_plant}: reset {jjdxr_fundus} to known commit, verify working tree state
177	* {jjdo_relay} + {jjdo_check} (timeout=0): launch async job, instant probe shows {jjdxpe_running}
178	* {jjdo_relay} + {jjdo_check} (timeout>0): launch short job, await completion, verify {jjdxpe_stopped} + exit code
179	* {jjdo_check} file list: verify temp dir contents returned on terminal status
180	* {jjdo_fetch}: read specific file from {jjdxr_fundus} using absolute path from `BURX_TEMP_DIR`
181	* `BURX_LABEL` correlation: verify {jjdt_pensum} token round-trips through `BURE_LABEL` -> `BURX_LABEL`
182	
183	*{jjdxf_nokey} — bind auth failure:*
184	
185	* {jjdo_bind} returns non-zero exit status
186	* Error message indicates authentication failure
187	
188	*{jjdxf_norepo} — bind probe failure:*
189	
190	* {jjdo_bind} returns non-zero exit status
191	* Error message indicates {jjdt_reldir} validation failure (Layer 3 per {jjdk_reldir_safety})
192	
193	*{jjdxf_nogit} — plant failure, send works:*
194	
195	* {jjdo_bind} succeeds (SSH works, {jjdt_reldir} exists, BUK installed)
196	* {jjdo_send} succeeds (BUD dispatch does not require git)
197	* {jjdo_plant} returns non-zero exit status (no origin to fetch from)
198

file.content:
Test infrastructure for validating the
{jjsogrd_remote_dispatch}
command surface across SSH boundaries.

A
{jjdxf_profile}
is a named account shape that declares
{jjdxr_fundus}
preconditions — what is configured, what is deliberately absent, and what assertions the profile supports.
Each profile corresponds to an OS user account (`jjfu_*`) that can be installed on any target machine.
Tests are run against a host specified at invocation time;
the same profile set works whether the target is localhost or a remote machine.
Each profile is
{jjdxf_preflight}-probed
before execution — missing preconditions skip cleanly, not fail.

[[jjdxf_profile]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxf_profile}::
A named account shape for
{jjdxr_fundus}
scenario testing.
Specifies an OS username (`jjfu_*`), expected precondition state, and supported test assertions.
All profiles share the same
{jjdt_reldir}
string; the precondition difference is what exists at that path under each account's home directory.
Two families: happy-path (full command coverage) and failure-mode (one broken precondition each).

==== Account Names

All test accounts use the `jjfu_` prefix (JJ Fundus User).
The prefix is globally unique in the minting universe, collision-proof, and grepping a machine for `jjfu_*` accounts shows exactly what is installed for
{jjsogrd_remote_dispatch}
testing.

[cols="1,2"]
|===
| Account | Precondition shape

| `jjfu_full`
| SSH keys, project at {jjdt_reldir}, BUK installed, git origin configured

| `jjfu_nokey`
| OS account exists, no SSH key access from {jjdxr_curia}

| `jjfu_norepo`
| SSH keys, no project directory at {jjdt_reldir}

| `jjfu_nogit`
| SSH keys, project at {jjdt_reldir}, BUK installed, no git origin
|===

==== Happy-Path Profile

[[jjdxf_full]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxf_full}::
Full happy-path account.
User: `jjfu_full`.
{jjdt_reldir}: `projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster`.
Full suite:
{jjdo_bind},
{jjdo_send},
{jjdo_relay}
(including parallel dispatch),
{jjdo_check}
(both instant probe and polled await),
{jjdo_fetch},
{jjdo_plant}.
Serialized as `jjfu_full` in fundus OS account name.

==== Failure-Mode Profiles

Failure-mode profiles each break exactly one
{jjdxr_fundus}
precondition to validate a specific error path.

[[jjdxf_nokey]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxf_nokey}::
No SSH key configured for this account.
User: `jjfu_nokey`.
Expected:
{jjdo_bind}
fails with authentication error.
Serialized as `jjfu_nokey` in fundus OS account name.

[[jjdxf_norepo]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxf_norepo}::
SSH access works but no project at
{jjdt_reldir}.
User: `jjfu_norepo`.
Expected:
{jjdo_bind}
fails at Layer 3 probe per
{jjdk_reldir_safety}
(resolved-path check fails).
Serialized as `jjfu_norepo` in fundus OS account name.

[[jjdxf_nogit]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxf_nogit}::
SSH access and project directory exist, but git remote is not configured.
User: `jjfu_nogit`.
{jjdt_reldir}:
directory exists with BUK installed, but `git remote` returns no origin.
Expected:
{jjdo_plant}
fails (no origin to fetch from).
{jjdo_send}
still works (BUD dispatch does not require git).
Serialized as `jjfu_nogit` in fundus OS account name.

==== Preflight Contract

[[jjdxf_preflight]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxf_preflight}::
Probe executed before running a
{jjdxf_profile_p}
test.
If preconditions are not met, the test fails with a diagnostic message directing the operator to provision accounts.
Tests must never silently pass when preconditions are absent — a passing test means the assertion was actually exercised.
+
Each profile requires a corresponding {xref_BURN} on the
{jjdxr_curia}.
The BURN alias is the addressing handle — preflight probes connect via the alias, not raw `user@host`.
Test account BURN profiles carry `BURN_COMMAND` appropriate to the target platform (empty for Linux/macOS default shell, platform-specific for Windows subsystems).

*{jjdxf_full} profile:*

. BURN resolution: kindle `jjfu_full-<suffix>/burn.env`, extract alias
. SSH connection: `ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o BatchMode=yes <BURN_ALIAS> exit 0`
. {jjdt_reldir} existence: `ssh <BURN_ALIAS> test -d $HOME/RELDIR`
. BUK installation: `ssh <BURN_ALIAS> test -f $HOME/RELDIR/.buk/burc.env`
. Any step fails -> test fails with provisioning instructions

*Failure-mode profiles* ({jjdxf_nokey}, {jjdxf_norepo}, {jjdxf_nogit}):

. BURN profile must exist for the account (provides alias and host coordinates)
. Profile-specific precondition check via BURN alias (SSH reachability, directory state)
. Missing precondition -> test fails with provisioning instructions
. These profiles deliberately fail at different stages — the
{jjdxf_preflight}
checks only that the test can be meaningfully attempted

==== Tabtarget Surface

Suite colophon: `jjw-tfs`.
Single colophon: `jjw-tfS`.
Channel: imprint selects the target host.

*Suite tabtarget:*
`tt/jjw-tfs.TestFundusScenario.<host>.sh` — runs all profiles against `<host>`.

*Single tabtarget:*
`tt/jjw-tfS.TestFundusSingle.<host>.sh <test-function-name>` — runs one test function against `<host>`.
The argument is the exact Rust test function name (e.g., `full::bind_send`).

Each tabtarget:

. Sets `JJTEST_HOST` from the imprint
. Invokes `cargo test --test fundus_scenario -- --test-threads=1 --ignored [filter]`
. Individual test functions run their own {jjdxf_preflight} and fail if preconditions are not met

Test thread count is always 1 — tests within a suite are sequential
({jjdxr_fundus}
state is shared).

==== Test Assertions by Profile

*{jjdxf_full} — full happy-path:*

* {jjdo_bind} + {jjdo_send}: bind to {jjdxr_fundus}, run simple command, verify output
* {jjdo_plant}: reset {jjdxr_fundus} to known commit, verify working tree state
* {jjdo_relay} + {jjdo_check} (timeout=0): launch async job, instant probe shows {jjdxpe_running}
* {jjdo_relay} + {jjdo_check} (timeout>0): launch short job, await completion, verify {jjdxpe_stopped} + exit code
* {jjdo_check} file list: verify temp dir contents returned on terminal status
* {jjdo_fetch}: read specific file from {jjdxr_fundus} using absolute path from `BURX_TEMP_DIR`
* `BURX_LABEL` correlation: verify {jjdt_pensum} token round-trips through `BURE_LABEL` -> `BURX_LABEL`

*{jjdxf_nokey} — bind auth failure:*

* {jjdo_bind} returns non-zero exit status
* Error message indicates authentication failure

*{jjdxf_norepo} — bind probe failure:*

* {jjdo_bind} returns non-zero exit status
* Error message indicates {jjdt_reldir} validation failure (Layer 3 per {jjdk_reldir_safety})

*{jjdxf_nogit} — plant failure, send works:*

* {jjdo_bind} succeeds (SSH works, {jjdt_reldir} exists, BUK installed)
* {jjdo_send} succeeds (BUD dispatch does not require git)
* {jjdo_plant} returns non-zero exit status (no origin to fetch from)

# JJK Kit Source

Edits here apply to the kit source only. Do not modify the active installation in `.claude/` or the parent project's CLAUDE.md.

lastPrompt: Serious Job Jockey architecture discussion — decoupling JJ from the work repo.    Today JJ is married into each work repo (gallops committed alongside the code).   I want to move to: a central, single…

aiTitle: Decouple Job Jockey from work repositories

content.text: Now the substantive reads — the load-bearing sections of JJS0 plus the three Rust files in full.

content.content:
305	== Overview
306	
307	The
308	{jjdgr_gallops}
309	is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
310	It tracks
311	{jjdhr_heat_s}
312	(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
313	{jjdpr_pace_s}
314	(discrete actions).
315	
316	== Design Principles
317	
318	=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
319	
320	All access to the
321	{jjdgr_gallops}
322	goes through {jjdx_handler} functions within {jjdx_vvx}.
323	No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
324	This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
325	
326	The rationale: Rust {jjdx_handler} functions replace fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
327	The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
328	
329	=== Git as Journal
330	
331	The
332	{jjdgr_gallops}
333	remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
334	Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
335	Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
336	
337	Git has the full history of every mutation.
338	Plan evolution is visible through commit diffs without additional in-JSON bookkeeping.
339	
340	=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
341	
342	The
343	{jjdhm_order}
344	array contains full
345	{jjdt_coronet_s}.
346	Each
347	{jjdt_coronet}
348	embeds its parent
349	{jjdt_firemark}
350	(redundant within a single
351	{jjdhr_heat}).
352	This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
353	
354	=== Crash-Safe Architecture
355	
356	Every operation follows the same invariant regardless of transport:
357	lock → load → transform → save → unlock.
358	+
359	The {jjdx_handler} acquires a git-ref lock (`git update-ref`),
360	loads the {jjdgr_gallops} JSON,
361	performs the transformation,
362	writes atomically (temp file then rename),
363	and releases the lock.
364	If the {jjdx_handler} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
365	+
366	No in-memory {jjdgr_gallops} state persists between operations.
367	Each operation reads from disk and writes back.
368	This is critical for the {jjdx_mcp}, which is a long-lived process:
369	caching state between calls would produce stale reads when multiple sessions write concurrently.
370	
371	=== MCP Transport
372	
373	[[jjdx_mcp]]
374	//axvo_procedure axd_longrunning
375	{jjdx_mcp}::
376	The `vvx mcp` subcommand starts a long-lived stdio server implementing the Model Context Protocol (MCP) specification version 2025-11-25.
377	The server communicates via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.
378	Implemented using the `rmcp` crate (official Rust MCP SDK), version pinned exactly.
379	+
380	The {jjdx_mcp} process is stateless infrastructure — it does not manage {jjdxo_officium} lifecycle.
381	{jjdxo_officium_s} are created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}), not by server process startup.
382	See <<jjdxo_officium>> for lifecycle details.
383	
384	[[jjdx_tool]]
385	//axl_voices axo_entity
386	{jjdx_tool}::
387	A JSON-RPC tool call within an {jjdx_mcp} session.
388	Each operation is registered as an {jjdx_tool} with typed parameter schemas and descriptions.
389	{jjdx_tool_s} are the sole invocation path for Claude Code.
390	The {jjdx_cli} CLI subcommand path is removed;
391	{jjdx_tool_s} are NOT also available as CLI subcommands.
392	+
393	Infrastructure commands ({jjdx_vvx} emplace, unlock) remain CLI-only.
394	
395	[[jjdx_handler]]
396	//axl_voices axo_entity
397	{jjdx_handler}::
398	The transport-agnostic Rust function that implements an operation.
399	Both {jjdx_tool_s} and (historical) CLI subcommands invoke the same {jjdx_handler}.
400	{jjdx_handler} functions accept typed parameters and return structured results;
401	the transport layer (MCP or CLI) handles serialization.
402	
403	Each {jjdx_tool} call is stateless: the {jjdx_handler} performs the full crash-safe invariant (lock → load → transform → save → unlock) per invocation.
404	The {jjdx_mcp} holds no {jjdgr_gallops} state between calls.
405	+
406	Operations that previously read text from stdin (
407	{jjdo_enroll},
408	{jjsoprd_revise_docket},
409	{jjdo_transfer},
410	{jjdo_close},
411	{jjdo_landing})
412	accept structured string parameters instead.
413	The {jjdx_mcp} resolves the {jjdgr_gallops} path internally;
414	{jjda_file} is not exposed as a tool parameter.
415	
416	=== Officium Lifecycle
417	
418	[[jjdxo_officium]]
419	//axve_entity
420	{jjdxo_officium}::
421	A bounded agent session with a unique identity and a dedicated filesystem directory.
422	Each chat session has exactly one {jjdxo_officium}, created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}).
423	+
424	The {jjdxo_officium} is decoupled from {jjdx_mcp} process lifetime.
425	The {jjdx_mcp} is stateless disposable infrastructure — it may restart at any time without affecting the {jjdxo_officium}.
426	The agent (chat session) is the stable identity anchor; the {jjdxo_officium} ID is a routing parameter passed on every {jjdx_tool} call.
427	+
428	The term derives from the Latin _Officium Divinum_ (Divine Office) — a structured, bounded period of intentional work.
429	+
430	Multiple {jjdxo_officium_s} may coexist concurrently (multiple Claude Code windows).
431	Per {jjdk_sole_operator}, all belong to a single operator.
432	The commit lock serializes their mutations; the {jjdxo_exchange} isolates their gazette file I/O.
433	
434	==== Identity
435	
436	Each {jjdxo_officium} is assigned a unique identifier by {jjdo_open}.
437	Format: `YYMMDD-NNNN` where `YYMMDD` is the local datestamp and `NNNN` is an autonumber (enumerate existing directories for the date, pick next unused starting at 1000).
438	No persistent counter file — the directory listing is the source of truth.
439	+
440	Unicode verification prefix: ☉ (U+2609 SUN, evoking the Divine Office's daily cycle).
441	The ☉ appears in parameters and display; it is stripped for the directory name.
442	This parallels the ₣/₢ convention for {jjdt_firemark_s} and {jjdt_coronet_s}.
443	+
444	The identifier appears in the {jjdxo_exchange} path and in the {jjdxo_invitatory} commit.
445	
446	==== Directory Layout
447	
448	Each {jjdxo_officium} creates a directory via {jjdo_open}:
449	+
450	----
451	.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/gazette_in.md   (ephemeral: agent → server)
452	.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/gazette_out.md  (ephemeral: server → agent)
453	.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/heartbeat
454	----
455	+
456	The `officia/` directory is the parent for all active {jjdxo_officium_s}.
457	Gazette files (`gazette_in.md`, `gazette_out.md`) have single-MCP-call lifetime — they are parameters and return values, not persistent state.
458	The {jjdxo_heartbeat} file provides liveness detection.
459	+
460	The entire `.claude/jjm/officia/` tree is gitignored — it is ephemeral exchange infrastructure, not tracked state.
461	This eliminates git noise from officium operations and makes incorrect {jjdxo_exsanguination} benign (no tracked files lost).
462	+
463	Directories that survive session termination are stale and are reaped by {jjdxo_exsanguination} at the next {jjdo_open}.
464	
465	[[jjdxo_exchange]]
466	//axve_entity
467	{jjdxo_exchange}::
468	The per-{jjdxo_officium} directory (`.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/`) used for {jjsz_gazette} file exchange between {jjdx_tool_s} and Claude.
469	+
470	Only one {jjsz_gazette} file exists at a time: {jjdxo_gazette}.
471	Operations that produce gazette output write to this file; Claude reads it.
472	Claude writes gazette input to this file; operations that accept gazette input read it.
473	+
474	The sequential nature of tool calls (one at a time per session) means the single file never has concurrent readers and writers within one {jjdxo_officium}.
475	
476	[[jjdxo_gazette]]
477	//axve_entity
478	{jjdxo_gazette}::
479	Two directional gazette files within an {jjdxo_exchange}: `gazette_in.md` (agent to server) and `gazette_out.md` (server to agent).
480	Contain {jjsz_gazette} wire-format markdown.
481	Both have single-MCP-call lifetime — every {jjdx_tool_s} call deletes both files on entry before dispatch.
482	+
483	`gazette_in.md` is written by Claude before setter operations ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjsoprd_revise_docket}, {jjdo_paddock} setter).
484	`gazette_out.md` is written by the server after getter operations ({jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_show}, {jjdo_paddock} getter).
485	
486	[[jjdxo_invitatory]]
487	//axvo_procedure axd_internal
488	{jjdxo_invitatory}::
489	The officium creation procedure.
490	Triggered by the agent calling {jjdo_open} — not by {jjdx_mcp} process startup.
491	The agent calls {jjdo_open} once per chat session.
492	+
493	{jjds_behavior}
494	+
495	. Run {jjdxo_exsanguination} (reap stale officia before creating new one)
496	. Generate {jjdxo_officium} identity (datestamp + autonumber)
497	. Create {jjdxo_exchange} directory with {jjdxo_gazette} and {jjdxo_heartbeat} files (both empty)
498	. Resolve current Claude Code session UUID by listing `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl` and selecting newest mtime (encoding rule: `/` and `_` both map to `-` in the absolute cwd path); fail loud on missing dir, zero `.jsonl` files, or mtime tie
499	. Collect model inventory via `vvcp_probe()` if daily probe has not yet run (check `.claude/jjm/officia/.probe_date` datestamp file)
500	. Create invitatory commit (action code `i`, `jjb`-branded) with `session:` + `cwd:` lines always present in body, optionally followed by model inventory when the daily probe ran
501	. Return {jjdxo_officium} identity string (e.g. `☉260327-1000`)
502	+
503	The agent receives the identity and passes it as a parameter on all subsequent {jjdx_tool} calls.
504	
505	[[jjdxo_compline]]
506	//axve_entity axd_retired
507	{jjdxo_compline}::
508	Retired.
509	The original design called for a clean shutdown procedure at {jjdx_mcp} process termination.
510	This proved unworkable: {jjdx_mcp} processes restart at unpredictable frequency, decoupled from chat session boundaries.
511	+
512	Staleness is now detected by {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime absence.
513	Cleanup is performed by {jjdxo_exsanguination} at the next {jjdo_open}.
514	Sessions simply stop — no compline runs.
515	+
516	The action code `o` is reserved-unused.
517	
518	[[jjdxo_heartbeat]]
519	//axve_entity
520	{jjdxo_heartbeat}::
521	A sentinel file within the {jjdxo_exchange} directory: `heartbeat`.
522	Every {jjdx_tool} dispatch touches this file (updates mtime).
523	+
524	The {jjdxo_heartbeat} provides liveness detection without coupling to process trees or Claude Code internals.
525	A stale {jjdxo_heartbeat} (mtime exceeding the staleness threshold) indicates the owning session has ended.
526	{jjdxo_exsanguination} uses {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime to identify directories safe to reap.
527	
528	[[jjdxo_exsanguination]]
529	//axvo_procedure axd_internal
530	{jjdxo_exsanguination}::
531	The stale officium reaping procedure.
532	Runs at the beginning of each {jjdxo_invitatory}, before creating the new {jjdxo_officium} directory.
533	Returns counts of active and reaped officia for {jjdo_open} summary output.
534	+
535	{jjds_behavior}
536	+
537	. Scan `.claude/jjm/officia/` for directories (skip hidden entries)
538	. For each directory, check {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime
539	. Remove directories whose {jjdxo_heartbeat} exceeds the staleness threshold (7 days); count as reaped
540	. Count directories within threshold as active
541	. Skip directories with no {jjdxo_heartbeat} file (legacy or corrupt — log warning, leave for manual cleanup)
542	. Return (active count, reaped count)
543	+
544	The week-long staleness threshold ensures all same-day directories survive long enough for the autonumber scan to see them, preventing {jjdxo_officium} identity collisions within a day.
545	Because the `officia/` tree is gitignored, incorrect reaping is benign — no tracked files are lost.
546	
547	==== Steeplechase Commit Patterns
548	
549	{jjdxo_invitatory} commit (action code `i`):
550	+
551	----
552	jjb:BRAND::i: OFFICIUM <id>
553	
554	session: <uuid>
555	cwd: <absolute-path>
556	haiku: <model-id>
557	sonnet: <model-id>
558	opus: <model-id>
559	host: <hostname>
560	platform: <platform>
561	----
562	+
563	The `session:` and `cwd:` lines appear in *every* invitatory body — each chat session has its own UUID and may run from a different cwd, so the recall anchor must be present on every {jjdo_open}.
564	The model/host/platform inventory below them appears only on the first {jjdxo_invitatory} of the day (gated by `.probe_date`); subsequent same-day invitatories carry only `session:` + `cwd:`.
565	+
566	*Recall workflow.*
567	The `session:` and `cwd:` lines complete the chat-level recall loop:
568	+
569	* `git log --all --grep='OFFICIUM <id>'` — locate every commit from a given {jjdxo_officium} (subject anchor)
570	* `git log --all --grep='session: <uuid>'` — locate every commit from a given chat session (transcript anchor)
571	* To resume a chat: extract `session:` and `cwd:` from the {jjdxo_invitatory} commit body, change to that working directory, run `claude --resume <session-uuid>`
572	+
573	The `cwd:` line is recorded verbatim from the MCP server's process working directory at {jjdo_open} time — no symlink resolution, no case normalization. Recall is therefore machine-local; the transcript files under `~/.claude/projects/` are local anyway.
574	+
575	The invitatory commit uses empty identity (no firemark or coronet) — the officium is infrastructure, not affiliated with a heat.
576	+
577	The {jjdxo_compline} action code `o` is reserved-unused.
578	No compline commits are created — sessions end without ceremony.
579	
580	=== Deterministic Serialization
581	
582	Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
583	For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
584	This ensures that reordering paces via
585	{jjdo_reorder}
586	produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
587	+
588	The `heats` object uses BTreeMap (alphabetical key order).
589	{jjdgm_order}
590	is the authoritative source for heat display and selection order.
591	Operations that need heat order (
592	{jjdo_list},
593	default heat selection in
594	{jjdo_orient})
595	iterate
596	{jjdgm_order},
597	not the `heats` object directly.
598	
599	=== Identity Encoding
600	
601	Job Jockey uses the
602	{jjdt_insignia}
603	type system for all entity identities.
604	{axd_algebraic}
605	{jjdt_insignia_s}
606	({jjdt_firemark},
607	{jjdt_coronet},
608	{jjdt_pensum})
609	use the {jjdz_charset} providing compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
610	Digit count disambiguates type, with one exception:
611	{jjdt_pensum}
612	shares digit count 5 with
613	{jjdt_coronet}
614	and uses a `%` sentinel at position 3 for discrimination.
615	+
616	{jjdxo_officium}
617	uses
618	{axd_temporal}
619	encoding (YYMMDD-NNNN).
620	+
621	Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
622	See
623	{jjdt_insignia}
624	in the Types section for the complete identity table.
625	
626	=== Remote Dispatch
627	
628	Remote dispatch enables a
629	{jjdxr_curia}

file.content:
== Overview

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
It tracks
{jjdhr_heat_s}
(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
{jjdpr_pace_s}
(discrete actions).

== Design Principles

=== Exclusive JSON Ownership

All access to the
{jjdgr_gallops}
goes through {jjdx_handler} functions within {jjdx_vvx}.
No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.

The rationale: Rust {jjdx_handler} functions replace fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
The compiler is tireless; humans are not.

=== Git as Journal

The
{jjdgr_gallops}
remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.

Git has the full history of every mutation.
Plan evolution is visible through commit diffs without additional in-JSON bookkeeping.

=== Diff-Friendly Mutations

The
{jjdhm_order}
array contains full
{jjdt_coronet_s}.
Each
{jjdt_coronet}
embeds its parent
{jjdt_firemark}
(redundant within a single
{jjdhr_heat}).
This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.

=== Crash-Safe Architecture

Every operation follows the same invariant regardless of transport:
lock → load → transform → save → unlock.
+
The {jjdx_handler} acquires a git-ref lock (`git update-ref`),
loads the {jjdgr_gallops} JSON,
performs the transformation,
writes atomically (temp file then rename),
and releases the lock.
If the {jjdx_handler} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
+
No in-memory {jjdgr_gallops} state persists between operations.
Each operation reads from disk and writes back.
This is critical for the {jjdx_mcp}, which is a long-lived process:
caching state between calls would produce stale reads when multiple sessions write concurrently.

=== MCP Transport

[[jjdx_mcp]]
//axvo_procedure axd_longrunning
{jjdx_mcp}::
The `vvx mcp` subcommand starts a long-lived stdio server implementing the Model Context Protocol (MCP) specification version 2025-11-25.
The server communicates via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout.
Implemented using the `rmcp` crate (official Rust MCP SDK), version pinned exactly.
+
The {jjdx_mcp} process is stateless infrastructure — it does not manage {jjdxo_officium} lifecycle.
{jjdxo_officium_s} are created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}), not by server process startup.
See <<jjdxo_officium>> for lifecycle details.

[[jjdx_tool]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{jjdx_tool}::
A JSON-RPC tool call within an {jjdx_mcp} session.
Each operation is registered as an {jjdx_tool} with typed parameter schemas and descriptions.
{jjdx_tool_s} are the sole invocation path for Claude Code.
The {jjdx_cli} CLI subcommand path is removed;
{jjdx_tool_s} are NOT also available as CLI subcommands.
+
Infrastructure commands ({jjdx_vvx} emplace, unlock) remain CLI-only.

[[jjdx_handler]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{jjdx_handler}::
The transport-agnostic Rust function that implements an operation.
Both {jjdx_tool_s} and (historical) CLI subcommands invoke the same {jjdx_handler}.
{jjdx_handler} functions accept typed parameters and return structured results;
the transport layer (MCP or CLI) handles serialization.

Each {jjdx_tool} call is stateless: the {jjdx_handler} performs the full crash-safe invariant (lock → load → transform → save → unlock) per invocation.
The {jjdx_mcp} holds no {jjdgr_gallops} state between calls.
+
Operations that previously read text from stdin (
{jjdo_enroll},
{jjsoprd_revise_docket},
{jjdo_transfer},
{jjdo_close},
{jjdo_landing})
accept structured string parameters instead.
The {jjdx_mcp} resolves the {jjdgr_gallops} path internally;
{jjda_file} is not exposed as a tool parameter.

=== Officium Lifecycle

[[jjdxo_officium]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_officium}::
A bounded agent session with a unique identity and a dedicated filesystem directory.
Each chat session has exactly one {jjdxo_officium}, created by explicit agent action ({jjdo_open}).
+
The {jjdxo_officium} is decoupled from {jjdx_mcp} process lifetime.
The {jjdx_mcp} is stateless disposable infrastructure — it may restart at any time without affecting the {jjdxo_officium}.
The agent (chat session) is the stable identity anchor; the {jjdxo_officium} ID is a routing parameter passed on every {jjdx_tool} call.
+
The term derives from the Latin _Officium Divinum_ (Divine Office) — a structured, bounded period of intentional work.
+
Multiple {jjdxo_officium_s} may coexist concurrently (multiple Claude Code windows).
Per {jjdk_sole_operator}, all belong to a single operator.
The commit lock serializes their mutations; the {jjdxo_exchange} isolates their gazette file I/O.

==== Identity

Each {jjdxo_officium} is assigned a unique identifier by {jjdo_open}.
Format: `YYMMDD-NNNN` where `YYMMDD` is the local datestamp and `NNNN` is an autonumber (enumerate existing directories for the date, pick next unused starting at 1000).
No persistent counter file — the directory listing is the source of truth.
+
Unicode verification prefix: ☉ (U+2609 SUN, evoking the Divine Office's daily cycle).
The ☉ appears in parameters and display; it is stripped for the directory name.
This parallels the ₣/₢ convention for {jjdt_firemark_s} and {jjdt_coronet_s}.
+
The identifier appears in the {jjdxo_exchange} path and in the {jjdxo_invitatory} commit.

==== Directory Layout

Each {jjdxo_officium} creates a directory via {jjdo_open}:
+
----
.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/gazette_in.md   (ephemeral: agent → server)
.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/gazette_out.md  (ephemeral: server → agent)
.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/heartbeat
----
+
The `officia/` directory is the parent for all active {jjdxo_officium_s}.
Gazette files (`gazette_in.md`, `gazette_out.md`) have single-MCP-call lifetime — they are parameters and return values, not persistent state.
The {jjdxo_heartbeat} file provides liveness detection.
+
The entire `.claude/jjm/officia/` tree is gitignored — it is ephemeral exchange infrastructure, not tracked state.
This eliminates git noise from officium operations and makes incorrect {jjdxo_exsanguination} benign (no tracked files lost).
+
Directories that survive session termination are stale and are reaped by {jjdxo_exsanguination} at the next {jjdo_open}.

[[jjdxo_exchange]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_exchange}::
The per-{jjdxo_officium} directory (`.claude/jjm/officia/<id>/`) used for {jjsz_gazette} file exchange between {jjdx_tool_s} and Claude.
+
Only one {jjsz_gazette} file exists at a time: {jjdxo_gazette}.
Operations that produce gazette output write to this file; Claude reads it.
Claude writes gazette input to this file; operations that accept gazette input read it.
+
The sequential nature of tool calls (one at a time per session) means the single file never has concurrent readers and writers within one {jjdxo_officium}.

[[jjdxo_gazette]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_gazette}::
Two directional gazette files within an {jjdxo_exchange}: `gazette_in.md` (agent to server) and `gazette_out.md` (server to agent).
Contain {jjsz_gazette} wire-format markdown.
Both have single-MCP-call lifetime — every {jjdx_tool_s} call deletes both files on entry before dispatch.
+
`gazette_in.md` is written by Claude before setter operations ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjsoprd_revise_docket}, {jjdo_paddock} setter).
`gazette_out.md` is written by the server after getter operations ({jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_show}, {jjdo_paddock} getter).

[[jjdxo_invitatory]]
//axvo_procedure axd_internal
{jjdxo_invitatory}::
The officium creation procedure.
Triggered by the agent calling {jjdo_open} — not by {jjdx_mcp} process startup.
The agent calls {jjdo_open} once per chat session.
+
{jjds_behavior}
+
. Run {jjdxo_exsanguination} (reap stale officia before creating new one)
. Generate {jjdxo_officium} identity (datestamp + autonumber)
. Create {jjdxo_exchange} directory with {jjdxo_gazette} and {jjdxo_heartbeat} files (both empty)
. Resolve current Claude Code session UUID by listing `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl` and selecting newest mtime (encoding rule: `/` and `_` both map to `-` in the absolute cwd path); fail loud on missing dir, zero `.jsonl` files, or mtime tie
. Collect model inventory via `vvcp_probe()` if daily probe has not yet run (check `.claude/jjm/officia/.probe_date` datestamp file)
. Create invitatory commit (action code `i`, `jjb`-branded) with `session:` + `cwd:` lines always present in body, optionally followed by model inventory when the daily probe ran
. Return {jjdxo_officium} identity string (e.g. `☉260327-1000`)
+
The agent receives the identity and passes it as a parameter on all subsequent {jjdx_tool} calls.

[[jjdxo_compline]]
//axve_entity axd_retired
{jjdxo_compline}::
Retired.
The original design called for a clean shutdown procedure at {jjdx_mcp} process termination.
This proved unworkable: {jjdx_mcp} processes restart at unpredictable frequency, decoupled from chat session boundaries.
+
Staleness is now detected by {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime absence.
Cleanup is performed by {jjdxo_exsanguination} at the next {jjdo_open}.
Sessions simply stop — no compline runs.
+
The action code `o` is reserved-unused.

[[jjdxo_heartbeat]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxo_heartbeat}::
A sentinel file within the {jjdxo_exchange} directory: `heartbeat`.
Every {jjdx_tool} dispatch touches this file (updates mtime).
+
The {jjdxo_heartbeat} provides liveness detection without coupling to process trees or Claude Code internals.
A stale {jjdxo_heartbeat} (mtime exceeding the staleness threshold) indicates the owning session has ended.
{jjdxo_exsanguination} uses {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime to identify directories safe to reap.

[[jjdxo_exsanguination]]
//axvo_procedure axd_internal
{jjdxo_exsanguination}::
The stale officium reaping procedure.
Runs at the beginning of each {jjdxo_invitatory}, before creating the new {jjdxo_officium} directory.
Returns counts of active and reaped officia for {jjdo_open} summary output.
+
{jjds_behavior}
+
. Scan `.claude/jjm/officia/` for directories (skip hidden entries)
. For each directory, check {jjdxo_heartbeat} mtime
. Remove directories whose {jjdxo_heartbeat} exceeds the staleness threshold (7 days); count as reaped
. Count directories within threshold as active
. Skip directories with no {jjdxo_heartbeat} file (legacy or corrupt — log warning, leave for manual cleanup)
. Return (active count, reaped count)
+
The week-long staleness threshold ensures all same-day directories survive long enough for the autonumber scan to see them, preventing {jjdxo_officium} identity collisions within a day.
Because the `officia/` tree is gitignored, incorrect reaping is benign — no tracked files are lost.

==== Steeplechase Commit Patterns

{jjdxo_invitatory} commit (action code `i`):
+
----
jjb:BRAND::i: OFFICIUM <id>

session: <uuid>
cwd: <absolute-path>
haiku: <model-id>
sonnet: <model-id>
opus: <model-id>
host: <hostname>
platform: <platform>
----
+
The `session:` and `cwd:` lines appear in *every* invitatory body — each chat session has its own UUID and may run from a different cwd, so the recall anchor must be present on every {jjdo_open}.
The model/host/platform inventory below them appears only on the first {jjdxo_invitatory} of the day (gated by `.probe_date`); subsequent same-day invitatories carry only `session:` + `cwd:`.
+
*Recall workflow.*
The `session:` and `cwd:` lines complete the chat-level recall loop:
+
* `git log --all --grep='OFFICIUM <id>'` — locate every commit from a given {jjdxo_officium} (subject anchor)
* `git log --all --grep='session: <uuid>'` — locate every commit from a given chat session (transcript anchor)
* To resume a chat: extract `session:` and `cwd:` from the {jjdxo_invitatory} commit body, change to that working directory, run `claude --resume <session-uuid>`
+
The `cwd:` line is recorded verbatim from the MCP server's process working directory at {jjdo_open} time — no symlink resolution, no case normalization. Recall is therefore machine-local; the transcript files under `~/.claude/projects/` are local anyway.
+
The invitatory commit uses empty identity (no firemark or coronet) — the officium is infrastructure, not affiliated with a heat.
+
The {jjdxo_compline} action code `o` is reserved-unused.
No compline commits are created — sessions end without ceremony.

=== Deterministic Serialization

Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
This ensures that reordering paces via
{jjdo_reorder}
produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
+
The `heats` object uses BTreeMap (alphabetical key order).
{jjdgm_order}
is the authoritative source for heat display and selection order.
Operations that need heat order (
{jjdo_list},
default heat selection in
{jjdo_orient})
iterate
{jjdgm_order},
not the `heats` object directly.

=== Identity Encoding

Job Jockey uses the
{jjdt_insignia}
type system for all entity identities.
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia_s}
({jjdt_firemark},
{jjdt_coronet},
{jjdt_pensum})
use the {jjdz_charset} providing compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
Digit count disambiguates type, with one exception:
{jjdt_pensum}
shares digit count 5 with
{jjdt_coronet}
and uses a `%` sentinel at position 3 for discrimination.
+
{jjdxo_officium}
uses
{axd_temporal}
encoding (YYMMDD-NNNN).
+
Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
See
{jjdt_insignia}
in the Types section for the complete identity table.

=== Remote Dispatch

Remote dispatch enables a
{jjdxr_curia}

content.content:
626	=== Remote Dispatch
627	
628	Remote dispatch enables a
629	{jjdxr_curia}
630	(the invoking side — where the
631	{jjdxo_officium}
632	lives, where JJK runs, where the LLM works)
633	to execute operations on a
634	{jjdxr_fundus}
635	(the executing side — where BUD runs, where BURX is written, where tabtargets physically execute).
636	
637	[[jjdxr_curia]]
638	//axl_voices axo_role
639	{jjdxr_curia}::
640	The invoking side of a remote operation.
641	Where the
642	{jjdxo_officium}
643	lives, where JJK runs, where the LLM works.
644	The administrative court from which
645	{jjdt_legatio_s}
646	are sent.
647	+
648	{jjdxr_curia}-side
649	state: the
650	{jjdxo_officium}
651	directory holds active
652	{jjdt_legatio}
653	handles and
654	{jjdt_pensum}
655	seeds (per
656	{jjdhr_heat},
657	in `pensum_seeds.json`);
658	pulled-back logs are archived by
659	{jjdt_pensum}
660	token.
661	
662	[[jjdxr_fundus]]
663	//axl_voices axo_role
664	{jjdxr_fundus}::
665	The executing side of a remote operation.
666	Where BUD runs, where `burx.env` is written (per {xref_BUS0}), where the tabtarget physically executes.
667	Latin: _fundus_ — estate, landed property; the ground where labor happens.
668	+
669	{jjdxr_fundus}
670	preconditions: user account with SSH key access from
671	{jjdxr_curia},
672	project directory at `~user/RELDIR` with BUK installed,
673	functional BUD dispatch chain, git repo with origin configured.
674	+
675	{jjdxr_curia}-side
676	precondition: a {xref_BURN} for the target connection,
677	providing host, user, SSH alias, and public key material.
678	The alias is the addressing handle — all remote dispatch operations resolve
679	{jjdxr_fundus}
680	coordinates through the BURN profile.
681	
682	==== Legatio and Pensum
683	
684	Remote dispatch uses a two-tier identity model:
685	
686	{jjdt_legatio}::
687	Session handle (connection descriptor).
688	The meaningful identity is the target pair (alias, reldir); the alias resolves host and user from a {xref_BURN}.
689	The token is
690	{jjdxo_officium}-scoped —
691	a convenience handle, not a persistent record.
692	See
693	{jjdt_legatio}
694	in the Types section.
695	
696	{jjdxp_pensum}::
697	Job identity (dispatch record).
698	Globally unique
699	{axd_algebraic}
700	{jjdt_insignia}
701	that correlates
702	{jjdxr_curia}-side
703	dispatch records with
704	{jjdxr_fundus}-side
705	logs via `BURX_LABEL`.
706	See
707	{jjdt_pensum}
708	in the Types section.
709	
710	[[jjdxp_pensum]]
711	//axve_entity
712	{jjdxp_pensum}::
713	A single dispatched asynchronous operation on a
714	{jjdxr_fundus}.
715	Created by
716	{jjdo_relay},
717	probed by
718	{jjdo_check}.
719	Identified by a
720	{jjdt_pensum}
721	token that round-trips through `BURE_LABEL` on the
722	{jjdxr_fundus}
723	into `BURX_LABEL` in the
724	{xref_BUS0}
725	`burx.env` fact-file.
726	+
727	{jjdxp_pensum}
728	state on the
729	{jjdxr_curia}:
730	{jjdt_legatio}
731	reference and remote temp dir path (captured at relay time).
732	Multiple
733	{jjdxp_pensum_s}
734	may coexist within an
735	{jjdxo_officium}.
736	
737	==== Pensum Report States
738	
739	{jjdo_check}
740	returns a liveness report for each
741	{jjdxp_pensum}:
742	
743	[[jjdxpe_running]]
744	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
745	{jjdxpe_running}::
746	`burx.env` present, PID alive, no `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`.
747	The dispatch is still executing.
748	Serialized as `running` in pensum liveness report.
749	
750	[[jjdxpe_orphaned]]
751	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
752	{jjdxpe_orphaned}::
753	`burx.env` present, PID dead, no `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`.
754	The dispatch crashed without writing terminal status.
755	Serialized as `orphaned` in pensum liveness report.
756	
757	[[jjdxpe_stopped]]
758	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
759	{jjdxpe_stopped}::
760	`BURX_EXIT_STATUS` present.
761	The dispatch completed; exit code available.
762	On terminal status,
763	{jjdo_check}
764	additionally returns the temp dir file list.
765	Serialized as `stopped` in pensum liveness report.
766	
767	[[jjdxpe_lost]]
768	//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
769	{jjdxpe_lost}::
770	`burx.env` file missing.
771	The dispatch's temp dir was not found at the stored path.
772	Serialized as `lost` in pensum liveness report.
773	
774	== Premises
775	
776	[[jjdk_sole_operator]]
777	//axk_premise
778	{jjdk_sole_operator}::
779	All concurrent {jjdxo_officium_s} belong to a single operator.
780	The commit lock serializes operations across parallel {jjdxo_officium_s};
781	cross-user concurrency is out of scope.
782	+
783	Consequently, every operation acquires the lock unconditionally — there is no
784	read-only fast path, and the {jjsodp_command_lifecycle} dispatcher need not
785	distinguish mutating from non-mutating operations.
786	+
787	{jjdxo_officium_s} need only identification (which session), not authentication (who).
788	
789	[[jjdk_reldir_safety]]
790	//axk_premise
791	{jjdk_reldir_safety}::
792	Remote dispatch involves destructive operations on the
793	{jjdxr_fundus}
794	(workspace cleanup via
795	{jjdo_plant}).
796	A malformed
797	{jjdt_reldir}
798	could resolve to `/` or `~`, destroying the filesystem or the user's home.
799	Three layers defend against this; no single layer is sufficient alone.
800	+
801	*Layer 1 — Rust constant validation (compile-time obvious):*
802	Must not be empty, must not start with `/` (absolute), must not start with `.` (traversal), must contain at least one `/` (forces subdirectory depth).
803	+
804	*Layer 2 — Fundus-side resolved-path check:*
805	The remote command validates the resolved path before any destructive operation: must be strictly under `$HOME`, must have minimum depth (at least 2 components under `$HOME`).
806	+
807	*Layer 3 — Bind-time probe:*
808	{jjdo_bind}
809	performs the same resolved-path check at session creation.
810	Fail fast before any
811	{jjdo_relay}
812	is ever attempted.
813	
814	== Equestrian Vocabulary
815

file.content:
=== Remote Dispatch

Remote dispatch enables a
{jjdxr_curia}
(the invoking side — where the
{jjdxo_officium}
lives, where JJK runs, where the LLM works)
to execute operations on a
{jjdxr_fundus}
(the executing side — where BUD runs, where BURX is written, where tabtargets physically execute).

[[jjdxr_curia]]
//axl_voices axo_role
{jjdxr_curia}::
The invoking side of a remote operation.
Where the
{jjdxo_officium}
lives, where JJK runs, where the LLM works.
The administrative court from which
{jjdt_legatio_s}
are sent.
+
{jjdxr_curia}-side
state: the
{jjdxo_officium}
directory holds active
{jjdt_legatio}
handles and
{jjdt_pensum}
seeds (per
{jjdhr_heat},
in `pensum_seeds.json`);
pulled-back logs are archived by
{jjdt_pensum}
token.

[[jjdxr_fundus]]
//axl_voices axo_role
{jjdxr_fundus}::
The executing side of a remote operation.
Where BUD runs, where `burx.env` is written (per {xref_BUS0}), where the tabtarget physically executes.
Latin: _fundus_ — estate, landed property; the ground where labor happens.
+
{jjdxr_fundus}
preconditions: user account with SSH key access from
{jjdxr_curia},
project directory at `~user/RELDIR` with BUK installed,
functional BUD dispatch chain, git repo with origin configured.
+
{jjdxr_curia}-side
precondition: a {xref_BURN} for the target connection,
providing host, user, SSH alias, and public key material.
The alias is the addressing handle — all remote dispatch operations resolve
{jjdxr_fundus}
coordinates through the BURN profile.

==== Legatio and Pensum

Remote dispatch uses a two-tier identity model:

{jjdt_legatio}::
Session handle (connection descriptor).
The meaningful identity is the target pair (alias, reldir); the alias resolves host and user from a {xref_BURN}.
The token is
{jjdxo_officium}-scoped —
a convenience handle, not a persistent record.
See
{jjdt_legatio}
in the Types section.

{jjdxp_pensum}::
Job identity (dispatch record).
Globally unique
{axd_algebraic}
{jjdt_insignia}
that correlates
{jjdxr_curia}-side
dispatch records with
{jjdxr_fundus}-side
logs via `BURX_LABEL`.
See
{jjdt_pensum}
in the Types section.

[[jjdxp_pensum]]
//axve_entity
{jjdxp_pensum}::
A single dispatched asynchronous operation on a
{jjdxr_fundus}.
Created by
{jjdo_relay},
probed by
{jjdo_check}.
Identified by a
{jjdt_pensum}
token that round-trips through `BURE_LABEL` on the
{jjdxr_fundus}
into `BURX_LABEL` in the
{xref_BUS0}
`burx.env` fact-file.
+
{jjdxp_pensum}
state on the
{jjdxr_curia}:
{jjdt_legatio}
reference and remote temp dir path (captured at relay time).
Multiple
{jjdxp_pensum_s}
may coexist within an
{jjdxo_officium}.

==== Pensum Report States

{jjdo_check}
returns a liveness report for each
{jjdxp_pensum}:

[[jjdxpe_running]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_running}::
`burx.env` present, PID alive, no `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`.
The dispatch is still executing.
Serialized as `running` in pensum liveness report.

[[jjdxpe_orphaned]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_orphaned}::
`burx.env` present, PID dead, no `BURX_EXIT_STATUS`.
The dispatch crashed without writing terminal status.
Serialized as `orphaned` in pensum liveness report.

[[jjdxpe_stopped]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_stopped}::
`BURX_EXIT_STATUS` present.
The dispatch completed; exit code available.
On terminal status,
{jjdo_check}
additionally returns the temp dir file list.
Serialized as `stopped` in pensum liveness report.

[[jjdxpe_lost]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value axd_string
{jjdxpe_lost}::
`burx.env` file missing.
The dispatch's temp dir was not found at the stored path.
Serialized as `lost` in pensum liveness report.

== Premises

[[jjdk_sole_operator]]
//axk_premise
{jjdk_sole_operator}::
All concurrent {jjdxo_officium_s} belong to a single operator.
The commit lock serializes operations across parallel {jjdxo_officium_s};
cross-user concurrency is out of scope.
+
Consequently, every operation acquires the lock unconditionally — there is no
read-only fast path, and the {jjsodp_command_lifecycle} dispatcher need not
distinguish mutating from non-mutating operations.
+
{jjdxo_officium_s} need only identification (which session), not authentication (who).

[[jjdk_reldir_safety]]
//axk_premise
{jjdk_reldir_safety}::
Remote dispatch involves destructive operations on the
{jjdxr_fundus}
(workspace cleanup via
{jjdo_plant}).
A malformed
{jjdt_reldir}
could resolve to `/` or `~`, destroying the filesystem or the user's home.
Three layers defend against this; no single layer is sufficient alone.
+
*Layer 1 — Rust constant validation (compile-time obvious):*
Must not be empty, must not start with `/` (absolute), must not start with `.` (traversal), must contain at least one `/` (forces subdirectory depth).
+
*Layer 2 — Fundus-side resolved-path check:*
The remote command validates the resolved path before any destructive operation: must be strictly under `$HOME`, must have minimum depth (at least 2 components under `$HOME`).
+
*Layer 3 — Bind-time probe:*
{jjdo_bind}
performs the same resolved-path check at session creation.
Fail fast before any
{jjdo_relay}
is ever attempted.

== Equestrian Vocabulary

1237	== Records
1238	
1239	=== {jjdgr_gallops}
1240	
1241	[[jjdgr_gallops]]
1242	//axl_voices axr_record_json
1243	{jjdgr_gallops}::
1244	The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
1245	Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
1246	
1247	==== Members
1248	
1249	* [[jjdgm_seed]]
1250	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_property
1251	{jjdgm_seed}
1252	is the next
1253	{jjdt_firemark}
1254	to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
1255	Incremented by
1256	{jjdo_create}.
1257	Serialized as `jjgrn_next_heat_seed` in JSON.
1258	
1259	* [[jjdgm_order]]
1260	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated axd_property
1261	{jjdgm_order}
1262	is the ordered list of
1263	{jjdt_firemark_s}
1264	defining heat display and selection order.
1265	Serialized as `jjgrn_heat_order` in JSON.
1266	Populated from sorted
1267	{jjdgm_heats}
1268	keys on first load of old-format files (migration);
1269	thereafter maintained explicitly.
1270	Updated by:
1271	{jjdo_create}
1272	(append new firemark),
1273	{jjdo_archive}
1274	(remove firemark via retain),
1275	{jjdo_continue}
1276	(move new firemark to position 0).
1277	{jjdgm_order}
1278	is the authoritative source for display and selection order, not
1279	{jjdgm_heats}
1280	key order.
1281	
1282	* [[jjdgm_version]]
1283	//axl_voices axr_member axd_optional axt_number axd_property
1284	{jjdgm_version}
1285	is the schema version number.
1286	Absent in V3 files; present in V4+ as an unsigned integer.
1287	Serialized as `jjgrn_schema_version` in JSON.
1288	{jjdr_load}
1289	detects migration mode when absent or when
1290	{jjdgm_order}
1291	is empty.
1292	
1293	* [[jjdgm_heats]]
1294	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_map axd_property
1295	{jjdgm_heats}
1296	{jjdt_firemark}
1297	{jjdhr_heat}
1298	is the dictionary of all
1299	{jjdhr_heat_s}
1300	keyed by
1301	{jjdt_firemark}.
1302	Serialized as `jjgrn_heats` in JSON.
1303	Serialized with lexicographic key order for deterministic output.
1304	Display and selection order governed by
1305	{jjdgm_order},
1306	not key order.
1307	
1308	=== {jjdhr_heat}
1309	
1310	[[jjdhr_heat]]
1311	//axl_voices axr_record_json
1312	{jjdhr_heat}::
1313	A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
1314	Contains zero or more
1315	{jjdpr_pace_s}
1316	representing discrete actions.
1317	Keyed in JSON by
1318	{jjdt_firemark}.
1319	
1320	==== Members
1321	
1322	* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
1323	//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_positional
1324	{jjdhm_firemark}
1325	is the
1326	{jjdhr_heat_p}
1327	primary key.
1328	Type:
1329	{jjdt_firemark}.
1330	Encoded as the JSON map key of this Heat's entry in
